text: hi I'm Phil from BBC learning english
today I'm going to tell you the
difference between even though and even
if now we use them both for emphasis but
they don't mean the same we use even
though to introduce something that's
true that might be a little bit
unexpected it's a bit like despite even
though I hate driving I'm still going to
Scotland by car even though I hate
tennis I'm still gonna watch the final
we use even if you introduce something
hypothetical so it might not be real
even if it's sunny when we're in
Scotland I'm not going swimming even if
you paid me I wouldn't watch that film
so even though is four things that are
true but unexpected and even if if the
things that are hypothetical so they
might not be real even though they look
the same they are different even if you
didn't notice at first
